title: Executive Order 11654—Transfer of the Federal Fire Council to the Department of Commerce

flags.justification: An advisory council transfer with broad, multi-department membership.
Sec. 5 requires the Council to report investigation and study results to the head of the concerned agency.
Multiple related functions all coherently serve one purpose: federal fire safety.
An advisory body with no binding compliance regime.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A standing advisory body for an ongoing function, evergreen by nature.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed; a routine agency transfer.
No third-party compliance regime is created.
Sec. 7 explicitly requires coordination with the newly-established Federal Advisory Council on Occupational Safety and Health, a well-designed cross-reference avoiding duplication.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
